original_query: select l_orderkey, sum(l_extendedprice * (1 - l_discount)) as revenue, o_orderdate, o_shippriority from customer, orders, lineitem where c_mktsegment = 'MACHINERY' and c_custkey = o_custkey and l_orderkey = o_orderkey and o_orderdate < date '1995-03-20' and l_shipdate > date '1995-03-20' group by l_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority order by revenue desc, o_orderdate limit 10;
rewritten_query:
SELECT
    l_orderkey,
    SUM(l_extendedprice * (1 - l_discount)) AS revenue,
    o_orderdate,
    o_shippriority
FROM orders
JOIN lineitem ON orders.o_orderkey = lineitem.l_orderkey
JOIN customer ON orders.o_custkey = customer.c_custkey
WHERE
    customer.c_mktsegment = 'MACHINERY'
    AND orders.o_orderdate < DATE '1995-03-20'
    AND lineitem.l_shipdate > DATE '1995-03-20'
GROUP BY
    l_orderkey,
    o_orderdate,
    o_shippriority
ORDER BY
    revenue DESC,
    o_orderdate
LIMIT 10;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Changed implicit joins to explicit JOIN syntax for better readability and to help the optimizer understand the relationships better
Reordered the joins to start with orders and lineitem tables first, then joining with customer, which may reduce intermediate result sizes
